name: Fudgy Tennessee Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9-inch pie plate with pastry. Flute the edge. Line with double thickness of foil. Bake at 450°F for 8 minutes. Remove foil. Bake an additional 4-5 minutes until crust is set and dry. When crust is done, reduce oven to 350°F. Set crust aside.
2. In a heavy saucepan, melt butter and chocolate together over low heat, stirring frequently. Remove from heat and cool slightly.
3. Meanwhile, in a large mixing bowl, beat eggs with an electric mixer on medium speed for 4-5 minutes until thick and light-colored. Beat in sugar, corn syrup, vanilla, and salt. Then, beat in the chocolate mixture.
4. Pour mix into partially baked pastry. Bake at 350°F for 35 minutes (center will still be soft). Do Not Overbake! Cool. Serve cool or warm with ice cream. Serves 8.
5. NOTE: For a brownie sundae, serve pie with your choice of toppings to spoon and sprinkle over the ice cream.
